tradeIdeas.title: Long Brent Crude / Long ASEAN Energy Producers
tradeIdeas.rationale: Hormuz effectively closed post-Feb 28 US-Israeli strikes. Brent at $85.60/bbl (Mar 6, +3.0%) — new crisis high driven by Iraq output cut of ~1.5M bbl/day; Kuwait/UAE may follow. Goldman models $18/bbl Iran shock add-on; BloombergNEF targets $91/bbl; J.P. Morgan upper-end $130. Qatar's 77 mtpa offline via force majeure (min. 1 month recovery). ASEAN energy producers (PTTEP, Medco Energi, Sapura Energy) benefit directly. Long near-dated Brent vs short deferred as backwardation steepens. Entered at $79; now $85.60; base target $106, stress target $125. US sub sank Iris Dena off Sri Lanka — Indian Ocean expansion raises tail risk further.

tradeIdeas.title: Long ASEAN Banks / Short ASEAN Energy Equities
tradeIdeas.rationale: ASEAN Banks at 208.1 (-2.5% Mar 6) — selloff accelerating as credit widens and Indian Ocean expansion raises systemic risk; MYR and SGD bank balance sheets relatively insulated vs PHP/IDR. ASEAN Energy Equities at 140.5 (+2.4%) bid on Iraq output cut and Brent $85.60. iTraxx Asia IG at 145bp (+7bp); ASEAN HY at 460bp (+16bp) — credit still widening. Maintain bank long for rate-cut delay beneficiaries. Wilmar and IJM plantation longs complement as oil-correlated ASEAN plays.

tradeIdeas.title: PHP Short — Elevated Conviction on Iran Shock
tradeIdeas.rationale: PHP most vulnerable ASEAN currency per MUFG. USD/PHP at 59.10 (Mar 6, +0.8%) — approaching 59.50 strike breakeven. 1,400+ OFWs requesting repatriation adds remittance uncertainty; Philippines formally rejected EDCA-base link to Middle East operations. >50% of PHL power from imported fossil fuels; no strategic LNG reserves. Current account deficit widens to -4.5% GDP at $90/bbl. BSP rate cut cycle on hold. JKM LNG at $17.80 (+6.0%) compounds import costs. MUFG stress target: 61.00. Stop-loss at 57.50.

tradeIdeas.title: IDR Short — Dual Exposure to Iran Shock and Shadow Fleet Risk
tradeIdeas.rationale: IDR at 17,050 (Mar 6, +0.8%). Net oil importer exposed to Hormuz-driven price surge — Brent at $85.60, Iraq supply shortfall compounding. Bank Indonesia rate hold at 4.75% despite growth pressure; ID 10Y at 7.40% (+4bp). Iraq cuts force Indonesia to seek dearer US/Australian crude alternatives. OFAC enforcement tightening on Indonesian-branded crude covering Iranian origin. Indian Ocean expansion (Iris Dena sinking off Sri Lanka) adds tail risk for crude rerouting. Target 17,500–18,000 in stress; 19,000 in tail. $100 Brent is now within a 17% move.

tradeIdeas.title: iTraxx Asia ex-Japan IG CDS Protection — Hold Position
tradeIdeas.rationale: iTraxx Asia ex-Japan IG at 145bp (+7bp Mar 6) — accelerating wider. Iran shock + Indian Ocean expansion (Iris Dena sinking) + Iraq supply cut creates triple-front credit deterioration. CDS entered at 102bp pre-shock — now +43bp in-the-money. P&I blackout fully in effect; 329 tankers trapped. Hold existing 5Y position; 180–220bp credible stress target in 1–3 month disruption. Sapura Energy and Hyflux bonds most vulnerable ASEAN HY names. ASEAN HY at 460bp (+16bp) accelerating wider.
instruments: Sapura Energy / Hyflux bond short via CDS

tradeIdeas.title: Long JKM LNG vs Short TTF — Spread Re-widening on Hormuz Shock
tradeIdeas.rationale: JKM at $17.80/MMBtu (+6.0% Mar 6) as Iraq cut of ~1.5M bbl/day compounds Qatar Ras Laffan force majeure (min. 1-month recovery). TTF at €55.40/MWh (+4.9%) — Atlantic Basin rerouting has structural limits; European storage drawdown risk rising. 84% of Hormuz LNG goes to Asia; JKM/TTF spread widening confirms Asian supply squeeze is structural. IRGC Malacca threat is extreme upside risk — Malacca disruption would also cut Australian LNG to NE Asia. Wilmar and PTTEP are client-linked expressions. In 1–3 month closure + Iraq cuts, JKM could approach $28–30/MMBtu.
instruments: Asia LNG spot cargo purchase via intermediary

cfTriggers.description: CRITICAL: Sapura Energy MYR debt maturities 2026–2027 face acute refinancing risk. iTraxx Asia IG at 145bp and ASEAN HY at 460bp signal the bank refinancing window is closing fast. High leverage (~MYR 10bn net debt), P&I blackout, and MAS FATF guidance compound risk. E&P revenue uplift at Brent $85.60 lags capex by 2–3 quarters. Engage bilateral bank discussions this week — window closes entirely if iTraxx breaches 160bp.

THB bond maturity H2 2026 — pre-finance at current 3.26% TH 10Y before BOT inflation repricing. THB at 32.10 and widening adds FX cost; 3.40%+ plausible if oil-driven inflation persists. PTTEP upstream EBITDA improves materially at Brent $85.60 — use the revenue tailwind to justify pre-financing now. MAS FATF guidance requires Gulf of Oman JV compliance review. Indian Ocean expansion (Iris Dena sinking) adds LNG and crude offtake shipping risk.

Expand USD revolving credit facility immediately to cover oil-driven input cost inflation. Bunker fuel ~12% of cost base — at Brent $85.60, annualised fuel cost delta vs Q4 2025 est. USD 390–450M. Biodiesel mandate tailwind (Indonesia B40, Malaysia B20) improves EBITDA but cash conversion lag creates working capital pressure. Pre-emptive RCF expansion of USD 500–750M recommended — iTraxx Asia IG at 145bp still executable for IG borrowers but closing.

Base scenario at 30% — Hormuz de-escalation could briefly push Brent toward $68–72, opening a refinancing window. At current $85.60 Brent and ASEAN HY at 460bp, capital markets are closed for unsecured debt. Explore private credit, DFI, or multilateral bridge financing as only viable near-term path. Engage restructuring advisor immediately — energy cost covenant must be reviewed vs plan assumptions at $55–60/bbl. The $25+/bbl breach is structurally material.
